mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above the 6.0 percent threshold signals a structural labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate consumer spending expectations, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in greater than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon through improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The industrial production index declining for two consecutive months signals acute supply-side bottlenecks or a sudden demand collapse, prompting short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in greater than 60 percent of consecutive decline instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.5 percent triggers market expectations of central bank policy normalization and higher discount rates, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 70 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as labor market strength translates into higher earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes against 60-day returns falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales indicates data revision risk or sectoral imbalance, prompting short-term portfolio rebalancing away from equities and generating negative returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with the GDP-sales divergence metric, or the t-statistic exceeds -1.5 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in industrial production and declining unemployment signals a synchronized economic recovery, boosting risk appetite and equity valuations through improved supply chain efficiency and labor market stability.
failure_condition: Co-occurring positive INDPRO and negative UNRATE changes fail to yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.
